The feature works correctly. The default env respects `MIXDOG_HOME`/`MIXDOG_DATA_DIR` (in this shell they point at a pristine temp dir with no history, hence `(none)`); pointing `--data-dir` at the real store confirms per-model aggregation and the grand total.

Created `scripts/usage-today.mjs` (+88 lines), no other files touched.

- Reuses `argValue` arg-parsing from `tool-failures.mjs`; supports `--data-dir`, `--json`.
- Resolves `agent-trace.jsonl` via `MIXDOG_DATA_DIR`/`MIXDOG_HOME` (fallback `~/.mixdog/data`), plus `cwd/.mixdog/data`.
- Filters `kind:"usage"` rows with `ts >= local midnight`; aggregates per-model `calls`, `input`(inputTokens), `cached`(cacheReadTokens), `output`(outputTokens), `costUsd`, then a `TOTAL` line.

Verify: `node --check scripts/usage-today.mjs` â†’ CHECK OK; ran once â€” with real store: TOTAL calls=170 costUsd=43.7372.

Note: usage rows here are `kind:"usage"` (carrying `costUsd`/`cacheReadTokens`), not the `usage_raw` rows, which lack cost.
